---
title: "Plots for JASA Presentation"
author: "Connor McNeill"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Map of Violent Crime in Philadelphia by Census Tract

```{r}
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Write your SQL query (no need for ST_X / ST_Y here)
sql_query <- "SELECT * FROM incidents_part1_part2 
              WHERE dispatch_date_time >= '2006-01-01' 
              AND dispatch_date_time < '2018-01-01' 
              AND text_general_code IN (
                'Homicide - Criminal', 
                'Homicide - Justifiable', 
                'Homicide - Gross Negligence', 
                'Rape', 
                'Robbery Firearm', 
                'Robbery No Firearm', 
                'Aggravated Assault No Firearm', 
                'Aggravated Assault Firearm'
              )"

# 2. Build the URL, but change 'format=csv' to 'format=geojson'
api_url <- paste0("https://phl.carto.com/api/v2/sql?format=geojson&q=", sql_query)
encoded_url <- URLencode(api_url)

# 3. Read directly into a spatial dataframe using st_read()
spatial_crime_data <- st_read(encoded_url)

# Now you can easily plot it!
plot(st_geometry(spatial_crime_data))

# Convert ucr code to integer
spatial_crime_data = spatial_crime_data |> 
  mutate(ucr_general = as.integer(ucr_general)) |>
  filter(ucr_general <= 400)

philly_tracts <- tracts(state = "PA", 
                        county = "Philadelphia", 
                        year = 2017, 
                        cb = TRUE)

crime_sf <- st_transform(spatial_crime_data, st_crs(philly_tracts))
crimes_with_tracts <- st_join(crime_sf, philly_tracts)

crime_counts <- crimes_with_tracts %>%
  # Group by the unique census tract identifier
  group_by(GEOID) %>%
  # Count the number of rows (crimes) in each group
  summarize(total_violent_crimes = n()) %>%
  # Drop the spatial geometry so it becomes a standard, lightweight dataframe
  st_drop_geometry()

philly_crime_map <- philly_tracts %>%
  # Merge the counts onto the spatial polygons
  left_join(crime_counts, by = "GEOID") %>%
  # If a tract had 0 crimes, it will show up as NA. Let's convert those NAs to 0s.
  mutate(total_violent_crimes = ifelse(is.na(total_violent_crimes), 0, total_violent_crimes))

ggplot(philly_crime_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = total_violent_crimes)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  labs(fill = 'Violent Crimes',
       title = "Violent Crimes per Census Tract, 2006 - 2017") +
  theme_void()

units(philly_crime_map$ALAND) = 'm^2'
units(philly_crime_map$ALAND) = 'mi^2'
philly_crime_map$ALAND = drop_units(philly_crime_map$ALAND)

philly_crime_map = philly_crime_map |>
  mutate(crime_density = total_violent_crimes / ALAND)

ggplot(philly_crime_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = crime_density)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(trans = 'asinh',
                       breaks = c(4, 30, 200, 1500, 10000)) +
  labs(fill = 'Violent Crime Density',
       title = "Violent Crime Density per Census Tract, 2006 - 2017") +
  theme_void()

ggplot(philly_crime_map) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = crime_density)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  labs(fill = 'Violent Crime Density',
       title = "Violent Crime Density per Census Tract, 2006 - 2017") +
  theme_void()
```
